minimal.summary: Architecture Studio for API design, database design, design-pattern selection, and system design with black-box boundaries, concrete artifacts, and migration-safe recommendations.

notes: wrapper surfaces are additive deployment forms, not capability types
orchestrators must not infer execution authority from wrapper presence alone

install_target.rationale: Source lives inside the current repository, so repo-local installation is the safer default.

minimal.rationale: Source carries both strong workflow/prompt structure and explicit agent-only control semantics. Keep one SSOT entry and emit both workflow and agent surfaces.

relationship_suggestions: prefer explicit review or analyst companions before high-authority use
pair with review and analysis capabilities when converging design proposals
migration_notes: If the capability lands, compare it against existing entries before apply.
Relationship suggestions are advisory only and do not imply runtime routing.
adjustment_recommendations: add an explicit primary objective before packaging
add deterministic in-scope sections to improve routing fitness
capture quality constraints or non-negotiables
quality_criteria: Score against the published architecture scorecard on every significant design branch
Include rejected alternatives and explicit trade-offs for major design decisions
Keep migration, rollback, and validation concrete enough to execute
Meet or exceed the Harish/Alexanderdunlop source bar and the local code-review/resolve-conflict bar

org_graph.work_graph_impact: Adds or updates a both capability with route profile SSOT_AUDIT.

ssot_sources.shared_summary: Architecture Studio for API design, database design, design-pattern selection, and system design with black-box boundaries, concrete artifacts, and migration-safe recommendations.
shared_constraints: Publish architecture guidance only; do not orchestrate or delegate work.
Use deterministic, sectioned outputs with explicit scorecards.
Prefer replaceable interfaces and stable boundaries over implementation-heavy advice.
Make assumptions, trade-offs, evidence, and rollback explicit.
Validate and report migration impact when designs change interfaces or ownership.

modes.mode_summary: Design resource-oriented APIs with explicit resource models, endpoint contracts, validation, security, and error behavior.
uplift_notes: Prefer nouns and stable resource boundaries.
Keep auth, pagination, and versioning explicit.
Include at least one explicit rollback path for API changes.

modes.mode_summary: Design schemas and storage structures that match access patterns, consistency goals, and evolution needs.
uplift_notes: Call out intentional denormalization and failure impact.
Tie indexes to actual query paths.
Include consistency-vs-availability trade-off and rollback checkpoint.

modes.mode_summary: Choose structural patterns that reduce coupling and improve replaceability under changing requirements.
uplift_notes: Explain trade-offs and why each fit is chosen.
Avoid pattern cargo-culting; always include replacement implications.
Include rollback plan for each non-backward-compatible boundary change.

modes.mode_summary: Design end-to-end architecture with explicit scaling, reliability, observability, and migration plan.
uplift_notes: Separate policy from mechanism in each subsystem.

ssot_sources.display_name: Testing Studio — Test Design and Coverage Analysis
ssot_sources.shared_summary: Testing family for unit tests, end-to-end tests, edge-case discovery, and coverage gap analysis.
shared_constraints: Generate or recommend tests only; do not execute them automatically.
Cover edge cases and failure paths, not only the happy path.

modes.mode_summary: Generate isolated, framework-aware unit tests for functions, classes, and modules.
uplift_notes: Prefer readable assertions and deterministic setup.

modes.mode_summary: Design end-to-end tests around complete user or system journeys.
uplift_notes: Call out test-data setup and synchronization explicitly.
Prefer critical user paths over broad UI clicking.

modes.mode_summary: Identify boundary conditions, invalid states, and failure modes missing from current coverage.
uplift_notes: Rank by business or failure impact.

modes.mode_summary: Analyze current coverage and identify what remains untested or under-tested.
uplift_notes: Tie missing tests to specific code paths or workflows.
Separate critical gaps from routine improvements.
